title: Sepsis Mortality Risk Score
study_design: Retrospective cohort study with model development and external validation

sample_size.source_quote: A total of 5,443 adult patients meeting the sepsis-3 criteria were analyzed, including 907 non-survivors and 4,536 survivors.

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: LASSO regression followed by logistic regression; AUC
mortality_predictors.cutoff_value: Low (<5%): 0–6 points; Moderate (5–20%): 7–11 points; High (20–50%): 12–14 points; Very high (>50%): ≥15 points
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The SMRS had good discrimination (AUC: 0.789) in the development set... SMRS was well-discriminated in the external validation set (AUC: 0.765)
SMRS was well-discriminated in the external validation set (AUC: 0.765), which was greater than APACHE IV and SAPS II (AUC: APACHE IV 0.754, SAPS II 0.751)
SMRS was well-discriminated in the external validation set (AUC: 0.765), which was greater than APACHE IV and SAPS II (AUC: APACHE IV 0.754, SAPS II 0.751)

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: AUC comparison across machine learning models
mortality_predictors.source_quote: All machine learning models, except the logistic regression model, showed good discrimination ability in the development set (AUC > 0.8). In the development and validation sets, the XGBoost algorithm achieved the best performance among the four models
